item.title: Learning From Multiple Tasks
item.description: Learning from Multiple Tasks Transfer learning Make use of a trained neural network on another task. If there are small amount of data for the new task, fix the trained parameters in the earlier layers, and re-initialize the parameters of the last few layers in the trained network. If there are more data for the new task, can choose to re-train the entire network. If re-train the entire network, the previous trianing is called pre-training, and the new training is called fine-tuning.

item.title: Mismatched Training and Dev/Test Set
item.description: Mismatched Training and Dev/Test Set The problem Sometimes, there are not enough data with the same distribution as dev/test set for us to use in training. Training data may then be obtained from other sources, but there is a potential issue of data distribution mismatch between training set and dev/test set. Bias Variance analysis when mismatched Apart from the avoidable bias problem and the variance problem, we now have an additional data mismatch problem.

item.title: Methods To Speed Up Training
item.description: Methods to Speed up Training Input normalization Performing input normalization on one sample: $$\bar{x} = \frac1m \sum_{i=1}^m x^{(i)}$$ $$x^{(i)} := x^{(i)} - \bar{x}$$ $$\sigma^2 = \frac1m \sum_{i=1}^m (x^{(i)})^2 $$ $$x^{(i)} := \frac{x^{(i)}}{\sigma^2}$$ Weight initialization In neural network, weighs need to be initialized randomly to break symmetry, and also need to be small to hit the region of large gradients in the activation functions. For tanh activation function, use Xavier initialization:

item.title: Bias and Variance
item.description: Bias and Variance Diagnosis of bias and variance Human-level performance error is often used as a proxy to the Bayes error (the theoretical lower bound of the error). Training set error is higher than the estimated Bayes error => avoidable bias Dev set error is higher than the training set error => variance Dealing with bias and variance Focus first on whatever is higher between avoidable bias and variance.

item.title: Neural Network
item.description: Neural Network Main steps for building a Neural Network Define the model structure (# of input units, # of hidden units, etc.). Initialize the model’s parameters / Define hyperparameters. Loop: Implement forward propagation Compute loss Implement backward propagation to get the gradients Update parameters (gradient decent) Often times, the steps are built separately and integrated together later on. Activation function choices Apart from the sigmoid activation function, there are a few more other choices.

item.title: What Happens Before Main
item.description: What happens before main()? For dynamically loaded x86 ELF files on Linux, what happens before main() gets to run? The following graph demonstrates the entire process, including things happened before main and after main. (It should be read in a depth-first manner.) How to get to _start? When a program is run, the parent process calls execve(). The execve() call sets up the stack, and pushes onto it argc, argv and envp.

item.title: ELF File on Linux
item.description: ELF File on Linux Introduction This post from Linux Audit offers a good introduction to ELF files. Details Refer to the ELF file reference document for detailed documentation of ELF file format. Useful tools To work with ELF file, there are a few useful tools: readelf: read information about different component of an ELF file. dumpelf: dump the entire internal ELF structure. vbindiff: find differences between two ELF files.

item.title: Intel X86 Opcode Table
item.description: Intel x86 Assembler Instruction Set Opcode Table Refer to this page .

item.title: Debugging Tips
item.description: Debugging Tips Some debugging tips and tricks with GDB and other tools. List all mapped memory regions 1(gdb) help info proc 2Show /proc process information about any running process. 3Specify any process id, or use the program being debugged by default. 4Specify any of the following keywords for detailed info: 5 mappings -- list of mapped memory regions. 6 stat -- list a bunch of random process info. 7 status -- list a different bunch of random process info.

item.title: Memory Mapped File Page Cache
item.description: Memory-mapped File and Page Cache Memory-mapped file A memory-mapped file is a segment of virtual memory that has been assgined a direct byte-for-byte correlation with some portions of a file or file-like resource. This file-like resource can be: (Typically) a file that is physically present on disk; A device; A shared memory object; (Some) other resource that OS can reference through a file descriptor. This correlation between the virtual memory space and the file permits applications to treat the mapped portion as if it were primary memory.

item.title: CVE-2014-9488
item.description: CVE-2014-9488 (less out-of-bounds read) The is_utf8_well_formed function in GNU less before version 475 allows remote attackers to have unspecified impact via malformed UTF-8 characters, which triggers an out-of-bounds read. More details are shown on CVE Details . Bug Detail The bin_file function in filename.c is called when using less to display a file to determine if the file is binary. It calls utf_bin_count function which returns the number of invalid UTF-8 sequences found in a buffer:

item.title: LD_PRELOAD Trick
item.description: Dynamic Linking and LD_PRELOAD Dynamic Linker A special part of an operating system that loads external shared libraries into a running process and then binds those shared libraries dynamically to the running process. Copies the content of libraries from disk to RAM, fills jump table and relocates pointers. Implementation in Unix-like systems An external executable (ld.so in Linux). At link time, path to the dynamic linker is embedded into the executable image.
